channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Judge rules against federal employees suing Trump admin for privacy concerns
item.description: A federal judge has ruled against federal employees who sued the Trump administration over privacy and security concerns around a government workforce email distribution system.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Elon Musk says millions in Social Security database are between ages of 100 and 159
item.description: Elon Musk indicated that a Social Security database lists millions of centenarians "with the death field set to FALSE.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump nominates Ed Martin, an ally who defended Jan. 6 rioters, to stay as top DC federal prosecutor
item.description: A conservative activist who echoed Donald Trump’s baseless claims of a stolen 2020 election and defended Jan. 6, 2021, Capitol rioters has been nominated by the Republican president to be the top federal prosecutor in Washington, D.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: GOP lawmakers divided on Musk, seen by some as a liability
item.description: Republicans on Capitol Hill are divided over tech billionaire Elon Musk being the public face of President Trump’s aggressive bid to shrink the government, with some souring on Musk’s prominent role as he increasingly has become a target of Democratic attacks.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Democrats grapple for response on education as GOP charges ahead
item.description: The Democratic Party is having to take a hard look at its education strategy as Republicans move aggressively on the issue with satisfaction in public education at its lowest point in decades.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: Washington Post backs out of 'Fire Elon Musk' ad order
item.description: The Washington Post this week backed out of a “Fire Elon Musk” advertising order that was to run as a wrap on some of its Tuesday editions, according to the advocacy group Common Cause.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, Fox 5

item.title: Trump moves on budget, policy cast shadow over Minnesota Legislature
item.description: The policies and priorities of President Donald Trump can be seen in St. Paul as lawmakers consider the spilldown effects. Meanwhile, protests of Trump across the country are planned with one happening in Minnesota on President’s Day.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: Trump begins firings of FAA air traffic control staff just weeks after fatal DC plane crash
item.description: The Trump administration has begun firing several hundred Federal Aviation Administration employees, upending staff on a busy air travel weekend and just weeks after a fatal mid-air collision in Washington.
item.dc:creator: Tara Copp, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: US leaders in Saudi Arabia for peace talks on Russia-Ukraine war
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said he will not accept a deal without a seat at the negotiating table.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: Republicans hope to jam through defense, border funding boost ahead of shutdown fight
item.description: Senate Republicans have the ambitious goal of jamming through funding boosts for defense and border security — separately from the appropriations process — ahead of a mid-March deadline to prevent a government shutdown. Senate Budget Committee Chair Lindsey Graham (R-S.C.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, Fox 5

item.title: Expert reveals what should happen next with Biden DOJ's lingering 'Jim Crow 2.0' election lawsuit
item.description: President Biden's lawsuit against Georgia's election system that he called "Jim Crow 2.0" is facing calls to be dropped by the newly sworn in Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump administration wants the Supreme Court to let the firing of whistleblower agency head proceed
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration wants the Supreme Court to permit the firing of the head of the federal agency dedicated to protecting whistleblowers, according to documents obtained Sunday that would mark the first appeal to the justices since President Donald Trump took office.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN and LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump says 14th Amendment is for former slaves, not migrants
item.description: On Thursday, a fourth federal judge blocked the executive order issued by the president on his first day in the Oval Office.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: House GOP budget resolution in jeopardy amid concerns from moderates
item.description: The House GOP’s budget resolution could be in jeopardy of not clearing the full chamber amid concerns from moderates over likely cuts to social safety net programs — particularly Medicaid — with a handful undecided on whether they will support the key measure. Reps. David Valadao (R-Calif.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The latest: Federal workers face mass layoffs
item.description: Federal workers all over the country have responded with anger and confusion Friday toward President Donald Trump’s aggressive effort to shrink the size of the federal workforce.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: 7 most likely GOP successors to Trump in 2028
item.description: President Trump raised eyebrows in an interview that aired Monday after he declined to name Vice President Vance as his automatic successor ahead of the 2028 presidential election. “No, but he is very capable,” Trump told fox News’ Bret Baier when asked if he viewed Vance as his 2028 successor.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, NewsNation

item.title: Trump officials signal potential changes at NOAA, the weather and climate agency
item.description: Federal workers at the home agency for the National Weather Service are concerned about a potential overhaul by Trump officials focused on cutting government costs.
item.dc:creator: Alejandra Borunda, Michael Copley, and Hansi Lo Wang, MPR News

item.title: University of St. Thomas scrambling after Dept. of Ed cuts millions in funding
item.description: The $6.3 million grant was targeted under President Donald Trump’s effort to cut programs the government considers “diversity, equity and inclusion,” or DEI.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer, Gretchen Brown, and Lukas Levin, MPR News

item.title: Immigration poll shows growing support for restrictions, but deep divisions remain
item.description: A new NPR/Ipsos poll shows growing support for some restrictions on immigration. Still, many elements of President Trump's sweeping crackdown are unpopular with Democrats and independents.
item.dc:creator: Joel Rose, MPR News

item.title: CA, 18 other states' AGs celebrate injunction against Trump's citizenship order
item.description: LOS ANGELES (KTLA)—California Attorney General Rob Bonta and 18 other state attorneys general have issued a statement after another judge blocked President Donald Trump's executive order seeking to terminate birthright citizenship.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5

item.title: OPM directs agencies to fire government workers still on probation
item.description: Office of Personnel Management (OPM) officials met with agency leaders across government Thursday and directed them to begin firing employees still in their probationary period a year or more after being hired.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: Minneapolis City Council postpones proposal to move violence prevention funds to county
item.description: After a lengthy and sometimes heated discussion, the Minneapolis City Council voted Thursday to indefinitely postpone a proposal to have Hennepin County temporarily oversee two of the city’s violence intervention programs.
item.dc:creator: Cari Spencer, MPR News

item.title: Legislature cancels meetings, closes building as Sierra storm arrives in Nevada
item.description: While Las Vegas drivers navigate rain-soaked roads for the first time in awhile, the Nevada Legislature has shut down for the day to keep drivers out of hazardous conditions in the Reno-Carson City area.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Nevada lawmaker moves to put Indigenous Peoples' Day on Columbus Day
item.description: Nevada law says Indigenous Peoples Day falls on Aug. 9, but a lawmaker is trying to change that with AB144, which would set the observation on the second Monday in October — the same day as Colombus Day.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Pope criticizes Trump administration's immigration policies
item.description: Pope Francis criticized President Donald Trump’s mass deportation plans in a letter urging compassion and the preservation of dignity for migrants in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: Trump seeks to expand his influence over arts and culture with Kennedy Center moves
item.description: After dominating U.S. politics for nearly a decade, President Trump now appears to be setting his sights on the country’s cultural landscape.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: Battleground Florida: Officials discuss tariffs, LGBTQ policies
item.description: University of Tampa Economics Professor Michael Coon explained the purpose of tariffs and the impact they will likely have on businesses and families in Tampa Bay.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump fires Office of Government Ethics chief
item.description: President Trump has fired the director of the Office of Government Ethics, the agency announced Monday. The office posted on its website that it had been notified Trump was removing David Huitema, who had been nominated by former President Biden.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WFLA, NBC
